title: Minesweeper
description: Classic Minesweeper with first-click safety, recursive flood-fill, right-click flagging, chronometer, localStorage best times, and three difficulty modes in a cyberpunk terminal aesthetic.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: First-click safety: mine planting deferred until first reveal; clicked cell + 8 neighbors guaranteed mine-free via safeSet coordinate map
Recursive flood-fill: for cells with neighborCount === 0, cascading reveal sweeps outward in all 8 directions stopping at numbered boundaries
Dual-input binding: click for reveal, contextmenu with preventDefault for flag toggle, both gated by gameOver flag
Tri-color number palette: n1=#00f0ff, n2=#00ff88, n3=#ff2a5f, n4=#a855f7, n5=#dc2626, n6=#14b8a6, n7=#1e293b, n8=#475569
Game-over reveal engine: all mines shown with bomb glyph, tripped mine gets @keyframes minePulse glow animation, wrong-flagged cells marked with orange cross
Chronometer: setInterval 1s tick starts on first click, 3-digit padded display, freezes on win or loss
localStorage msBest-{difficulty}: per-difficulty shortest time saved on victory, displayed in best bar on boot and after each win
3 difficulty presets: Beginner 9×9/10 mines, Intermediate 16×16/40 mines, Expert 16×30/99 mines with dynamic grid-template-columns
